answer:
**Vratisasana** (lit. "Ordinance of the Vows"), a Medieval Kawi prose text on ascetic (*viku*) discipline, primarily details the ongoing (*daśaśīla*, "ten virtues") vows of *Yama* (non-violence, chastity, truthfulness, non-engagement in worldly affairs, non-stealing) and *Niyama* (non-anger, devotion to the teacher, purity, lightness of diet, vigilance), to be protected "day by day" for mental steadiness and to avoid major transgressions (*pantən*).[^1] These appear lifelong rather than time-bound.[^2]

No temporary vows with fixed durations (e.g., lunar/solar years) are specified. However, two rules reference durations tied to vows:

- **Probation before consecration (*dīkṣā*)**: A teacher (*viku*) must observe a candidate's (e.g., son/disciple) conduct, rule adherence, and ability to bear the sacred vows (*yama-niyama*) for **four years** (main text) before consecrating them—ensuring they can sustain the ascetic state. Improper consecration incurs fines (6 *suvarṇa* 4 *māṣa*) or expulsion from Java.[^3] A variant appendix ('ga' lontar) extends this to **seven years**.[^4]

- **Eligibility to perform *dīkṣā***: Only after **sixty years** of age (fifty in appendix), with mastery of vows/rituals, is one fit to initiate others.[^5]

[^1]: *Vratisasana*, ch. 1 [VS_02j], [/works/unknown-vratisasana-d0fe75/01/translation/#p-c80289]. Yama/Niyama defined [VS_03-04][^2].
[^2]: *Vratisasana*, ch. 1 [VS_03s](#p-c93922)–[VS_04j§2](#p-2d4875), [/works/unknown-vratisasana-d0fe75/01/translation/#p-2d4875].
[^3]: *Vratisasana*, ch. 1 [VS_14j§2](#p-fb4092)–[p-4763c7], [/works/unknown-vratisasana-d0fe75/01/translation/#p-4763c7].
[^4]: *Vratisasana*, ch. 1 Appendix [VS_appendix-14j§1](#p-3a7f50), [/works/unknown-vratisasana-d0fe75/01/translation/#p-3a7f50].
[^5]: *Vratisasana*, ch. 1 [VS_11j§2](#p-b3eea5), [/works/unknown-vratisasana-d0fe75/01/translation/#p-b3eea5].
